fault_families.name: Low Flow / Low Pump Pressure / Pump Fault
fault_families.description: Generic circulation fault family for low flow, flow proving, pump low pressure, and pump fault alarms.
fault_families.manual_basis: Manual basis: pump running in reverse, valves closed, pump failed/verify pressure, and low flow linkage to low suction/freeze conditions.

root_causes.description: Pump not maintaining a fluid column or suction feed.

actions.action_text: Restore tank level.
Re-prime the pump.
Check suction-side leaks or loose fittings.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low tank level strongly supports loss of prime.
Very low amps suggest the pump is not doing hydraulic work.

root_causes.description: Entrained air causing unstable pump feed and poor head.

actions.action_text: Bleed air from the system.
Inspect suction piping for leaks or turbulence.
Check NPSH conditions and suction restrictions.

scoring_rules.explanation: Air in system strongly supports cavitation or unstable feed.
Low suction-side pressure can support cavitation or unstable feed.

root_causes.description: Flow restriction due to valve position.

actions.action_text: Verify isolation and process valves are open.
Check bypass valves and flow path.
Remove any unintended restrictions.

scoring_rules.explanation: Valve position directly supports a flow restriction.
Open valves reduce the likelihood of a valve-position fault.

root_causes.description: Pump/motor rotating in wrong direction.

actions.action_text: Bump motor and verify direction.
Swap leads as needed on 3-phase motors.
Recheck flow and pressure after correction.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low-ish amps with poor head can fit reverse rotation on some pump systems.

root_causes.description: Pump unable to generate expected head.

actions.action_text: Inspect pump head generation versus expected.
Disassemble and inspect impeller/wear surfaces.
Repair or replace pump as needed.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low outlet pressure supports poor head generation.

root_causes.description: Restriction starving the pump or circuit.

actions.action_text: Inspect and clean strainers.
Check suction-side restrictions.
Retest flow and pressure after cleaning.

scoring_rules.explanation: No flow condition supports restriction or blockage.
Low pressure rise can also appear with a severe restriction.

fault_families.name: High Head / High Discharge Pressure
fault_families.description: Condenser-side high pressure fault family for high head, high discharge, and poor heat rejection alarms.
fault_families.manual_basis: Manual basis: improper condenser cooling, clean air filters, check fan operation, high ambient, ensure adequate clearance, and verify process load.

root_causes.description: Poor heat rejection from fouled coil or blockage.

actions.action_text: Clean condenser coil or condenser bundle.
Remove debris from coil face and filters.
Retest head pressure after cleaning.

scoring_rules.explanation: Dirty condenser is a common direct cause of high head.
Clean coil reduces likelihood of dirty-condenser diagnosis.

root_causes.description: Fan off, wrong direction, or poor airflow.

actions.action_text: Verify fan operation and rotation.
Check fan motors, contactors, drives, and safeties.
Confirm condenser airflow/waterflow is restored.

scoring_rules.explanation: No fan operation strongly supports an airflow failure.
Poor condenser-side flow strongly supports airflow/waterflow issues.
Running fans reduce likelihood of primary fan failure.

root_causes.cause_name: High ambient / high condenser inlet temp
root_causes.description: Environmental condition limiting heat rejection.

actions.action_text: Measure ambient or entering condenser-water temperature.
Confirm conditions are within design range.
Reduce external heat load if possible.

scoring_rules.explanation: High ambient temperature increases condenser pressure.

root_causes.description: Hot discharge air re-entering condenser intake.

actions.action_text: Verify unit clearances.
Check for hot-air recirculation paths.
Improve ducting, spacing, or ventilation.

scoring_rules.explanation: Poor clearance can recirculate hot air back into the coil.

root_causes.description: Excess refrigerant backing up liquid in condenser.

actions.action_text: Verify subcooling against expected range.
Review service history for recent charge changes.
Recover/adjust charge only after confirming diagnosis.

scoring_rules.explanation: High subcooling supports backed-up liquid or overcharge.

root_causes.description: Gas contamination or condenser-side abnormality.

actions.action_text: Compare operating pressures to expected conditions.
Review evacuation and charging history.
Recover, evacuate, and recharge if confirmed.

scoring_rules.explanation: High head with high subcooling can still include condenser-side abnormality.
High head at low ambient raises suspicion of non-condensables or charge issue.

fault_families.name: Low Suction / Low Charge / Starved Evaporator
fault_families.description: Low-side refrigeration fault family for low suction, low charge, starvation, and liquid feed restriction alarms.
fault_families.manual_basis: Manual basis: low suction tied to low flow, low load, and temperature below fluid freeze point; expanded with generic low-charge and restriction patterns for cross-manufacturer use.

root_causes.description: System undercharged or depleted.

actions.action_text: Verify superheat and subcooling pattern.
Inspect sight glass and service history.
Do not add charge until leak/restriction checks are considered.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low suction is the primary symptom of low-side starvation.
High superheat strongly supports an underfed evaporator.
Low subcooling supports low refrigerant inventory.
Sight glass bubbles may support low liquid feed.

root_causes.description: Loss of charge from leak point.

actions.action_text: Inspect common leak points.
Use leak detector or soap bubbles as appropriate.
Repair leak before recharging.

scoring_rules.explanation: Observed leak signs strongly support charge loss.

root_causes.description: Restricted liquid feed to evaporator.

actions.action_text: Inspect filter dryer and liquid line temperature drop.
Check for restricted solenoids or valves.
Replace restricted component if confirmed.

scoring_rules.explanation: Normal/high subcooling with starvation supports a restriction.
High superheat can also fit a liquid feed restriction.

root_causes.description: Expansion device not feeding properly.

actions.action_text: Check TXV/EEV command and operation.
Verify sensor bulbs/probes and control inputs.
Inspect for sticking or failed metering device.

root_causes.description: Insufficient thermal load driving pressure low.

actions.action_text: Verify actual process load.
Check whether load is below minimum required.
Review need for hot gas bypass or control adjustment.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low load is a common alternate explanation for low suction.
Confirmed adequate load reduces low-load explanation.

root_causes.cause_name: Fluid below freeze point / freeze condition
root_causes.description: Low-side pressure depressed by freezing risk or low fluid temp.

actions.action_text: Verify actual fluid temperature and freeze point.
Check glycol concentration.
Inspect for low-flow conditions at evaporator.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low suction can accompany freeze-related low-side conditions.

fault_families.name: Freeze Protection / Freeze Point / Low Evap Temp
fault_families.description: Freeze protection fault family for freeze point reached, low evaporating temperature, and low fluid temperature alarms.
fault_families.manual_basis: Manual basis: verify glycol mix, compare fluid temperature to freeze point, and investigate low flow / low evaporating conditions.

root_causes.description: Freeze point too high for operating condition.

actions.action_text: Measure glycol concentration with refractometer.
Compare to target freeze protection.
Adjust mixture to safe operating concentration.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low glycol concentration raises true freeze risk.
Adequate glycol reduces low-glycol explanation.

root_causes.description: Localized freezing due to weak circulation.

actions.action_text: Verify pump operation and flow path.
Check valves, strainers, and DP.
Restore proper evaporator flow before reset.

scoring_rules.explanation: No confirmed flow strongly supports localized freezing risk.
Confirmed flow reduces low-flow explanation.

root_causes.description: Bad or unverified reading causing nuisance freeze alarm.

actions.action_text: Verify reading with a calibrated reference.
Inspect wiring and sensor mounting.
Replace/recalibrate sensor if needed.

scoring_rules.explanation: Unverified sensor reading raises suspicion of nuisance freeze alarm.
Verified sensor reduces sensor-fault likelihood.

root_causes.description: Control setting too close to actual operating limit.

actions.action_text: Review freeze protection setting.
Compare setpoint to real operating margin.
Adjust settings if application allows.

scoring_rules.explanation: A relatively warm freeze threshold can create nuisance trips depending on application.

root_causes.description: Fluid truly at or below the safe freeze point.

actions.action_text: Confirm actual leaving temperature.
Keep chiller off until freeze risk is cleared.
Restore flow/load/glycol before restart.

scoring_rules.explanation: Leaving fluid below freezing strongly supports an actual low-temperature condition.
Low evaporator temperature supports real freeze risk.

root_causes.description: Low load or unstable control driving evaporator too cold.

actions.action_text: Verify process load stability.
Review capacity control and differential settings.
Check for low-load hunting or unstable control.

scoring_rules.explanation: Low evaporator temperature can also come from low load or unstable control.

fault_families.name: Not Reaching Setpoint / High Leaving Fluid Temperature
fault_families.description: Performance fault family for high leaving temperature, poor pull-down, and unable-to-reach-setpoint complaints.
fault_families.manual_basis: Manual basis: high fluid temperature / high load, restart to pull down, verify compressor/cooling enabled, and review delay / fault settings where relevant.

root_causes.description: Actual heat load exceeds available cooling capacity.

actions.action_text: Verify actual process heat load.
Compare load to unit capacity.
Reduce load or confirm unit sizing/staging.

scoring_rules.explanation: Very warm return fluid supports high process load.
Large gap above setpoint indicates the unit is falling behind load.

root_causes.description: Poor heat transfer due to inadequate flow.

actions.action_text: Verify process flow and pump performance.
Check for restrictions, air, or bypassing.
Retest leaving and return temperatures after correction.

scoring_rules.explanation: Missing flow strongly supports poor heat transfer.
Confirmed flow reduces low-flow explanation.

root_causes.description: Cooling not enabled or compressor not running.

actions.action_text: Confirm cooling enable and compressor status.
Check safety lockouts and interlocks.
Restore compressor operation before deeper performance checks.

scoring_rules.explanation: If compressor is not running, no cooling is available.
Running compressor reduces primary no-cooling explanation.

root_causes.description: Low charge, fouled heat exchanger, or weak refrigeration performance.

actions.action_text: Check suction/discharge pattern, superheat, and subcooling.
Inspect condenser/evaporator cleanliness.
Check for low charge or compressor performance issues.

scoring_rules.explanation: If compressor runs but pull-down is poor, refrigeration performance loss remains possible.

root_causes.description: Available capacity not staged correctly.

actions.action_text: Verify stage enable logic and deadbands.
Check control outputs and stage transitions.
Correct staging sequence or control configuration.

scoring_rules.explanation: Improper staging can strand available capacity.
Correct staging reduces control-stage explanation.

root_causes.description: Measured temperatures not representing true system condition.

actions.action_text: Verify sensor locations and readings.
Compare with handheld measurements.
Correct sensor or interpretation issue before major repairs.

scoring_rules.explanation: Very small delta-T may indicate bad measurement or weak heat transfer.
